slides.title: Tell Me How
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 5 English, Unit 5: Tell Me How. Welcome to Unit 5! In this unit, you will explore the world of instructional and procedural writing. Procedural texts are everywhere around us. They tell us how to cook a meal, how to play a game, how to build something, and how to get from one place to another. Every time you follow a recipe, read a science experiment, or look at the instructions for a board game, you are reading a procedural text. By the end of this unit, you will be able to read, understand, and write your own clear and well-organised instructions. You will also learn an important grammar pattern called the zero conditional, which is used to describe things that are always true. This unit will strengthen your reading, writing, speaking, and listening skills, all through the lens... In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Read and understand procedural and instructional texts, identifying their key features and structure
Recognise and use imperative verbs (command words) such as cut, stir, place, and measure in your own writing
Identify and use time connectives (first, next, then, after that, finally) to sequence steps clearly
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to read and understand procedural and instructional texts, identifying their key features and structure. you will also recognise and use imperative verbs (command words) such as cut, stir, place, and measure in your own writing. you will also identify and use time connectives (first, next, then, after that, finally) to sequence steps clearly. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: communicate, quote, emphasize, persuade, convince, encourage. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Tell Me How: source text 1 and Tell Me How: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: What is the Zero Conditional?
bullets: The zero conditional is a type of conditional sentence that we use to talk about things that are always true. These include scientific facts, rules of...
slides.narration: The first language focus is What is the Zero Conditional?. The zero conditional is a type of conditional sentence that we use to talk about things that are always true. These include scientific facts, rules of nature, and general truths that happen every time a certain condition is met. We call it the “zero” conditional because there is zero chance that the result will not happen. If the condition is true, the result is always true. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: The Instruction Game (Pairs). Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
